ARD trustManifest + resolvable did:web

orank flagged "ARD Catalog valid but lacks trust manifest". The catalog already named did:web:orchestkit.yonyon.ai as its host identity, but that DID did not resolve. This PR makes the whole identity chain traversable, backing only what the site can actually prove.

The discovery chain an agent walks

/.well-known/ai-catalog.json host.trustManifest.identity │ = did:web:orchestkit.yonyon.ai ▼ /.well-known/did.json NEW resolves the did:web document │ verificationMethod -> publicKeyJwk ▼ /.well-known/http-message-signatures-directory same Ed25519 key (kid 6a120f4f14168a35) one key, two spec shapes, kept in sync

What the trustManifest advertises, and what it omits

FieldStatusWhy
identity + identityTypeadvertisedResolvable did:web, keyed to the published Ed25519 key
provenanceadvertisedpublishedFrom the open-source repo
signatureomittedNo request-signing bot; would be theater
attestationsomittedNo SOC2/HIPAA program to assert
trustSchemaomittedNo external governance framework
Click a row for the reasoning.

The served bytes







  
Honest triage of the full orank gap list

  

    
GapVerdictReason

    
      
ARD trust manifestfixed hereOn-site, verifiable. This PR.

      
Developer resources by "yonyon"largely off-siteOn-site levers (predictable URLs, llms.txt, titles) already pulled; brand-search for "OrchestKit" ranks #2. The deficit is entity authority under the "yonyon" name, an indexing/entity problem.

      
Wikipedia / Wikidata entityoff-siteRequires third-party notability and press. Not a codebase change.

      
ChatGPT app directoryoff-siteA GPT Store submission, external. Not a codebase change.

      
auth.md AS-metadata chainrefusedNo authorization server exists. PRM is honestly empty (anonymous-only). Publishing RFC 8414 AS metadata + agent_auth register_uri would fabricate a credential flow the site does not run.
